summary: Sprint B Slices 3+4 (Voita Telegram bot + Mittari signals subscription) — backend pytest 20/21 PASS, 1 SKIPPED (Voita raffle entry — no 'active' raffles available; all 5 returned raffles are status='paid' rejecting new entries). FE /mittari verified live: DialCockpit hero visually UNCHANGED (all 6 testids mittari-dial-slot/mittari-state-name/mittari-drivers/mittari-driver-streamers/mittari-method/mittari-optin present and rendering MITTARI · ROLLING composite=70 just like before); new [data-testid=mittari-signals] mounts BETWEEN drivers and method (verified by Y-positions: dial=150 < drivers=892 < signals=1101 < method=1254 < optin=1467); localStorage.putki_mittari_pending_id auto-generated as UUID on mount. Empty-state branch ([data-testid=mittari-signals-empty]) live-exercised since /api/odds/featured currently returns 0 picks (0 qualifying with sharpness>=40). Backend webhook coverage: (a) /help → kind=help, (b) chitchat → kind=fallback, (c) /start with no arg → start_no_pending, (d) /start <unknown_uuid> → start_unknown_pending, (e) /start mittari_<pid> after /api/mittari/subscribe → kind=mittari_bound, already_bound=False; binding-status flips bound=True with bound_at set; second /start same chat → already_bound=True (idempotent), (f) /start mittari_<pid> without pre-subscribe → still binds via upsert, (g) /stop and /unsubscribe → kind=stop, deactivated>=1, binding-status flips active=False (bound stays True since chat_id persists), (h) edited_message / unsupported update shapes return ok=true gracefully (handled=False), (i) /api/admin/telegram/set-webhook + /webhook-info + /bound-entries + /admin/mittari/subscribers all gated (403 without X-Admin-Token, 200 with putki-hq-admin). broadcast_mittari_state_change + send_post_match_result + handle_update + set_webhook + get_webhook_info all confirmed exported from telegram_bot module. Mocked Telegram sendMessage failure (chat_not_found on fake chat_ids) handled gracefully — does NOT block binding persistence. With-picks branch (5 cards, blur on cards 1-4, CTA click → /api/mittari/subscribe → unlock → 60s polling for bound badge flip) verified via code review of MittariSignals.jsx only — could not be live-exercised since /api/odds/featured.picks=[].
critical_code_review_comments: MittariSignals.jsx generates pending_id eagerly in useState initialiser (on every mount for first-time visitors) BEFORE the user ever clicks the CTA. This pollutes localStorage even for bounce visitors. Low-risk but consider lazy-generating on first CTA click instead.

telegram_bot.handle_update strips token_arg to 96 chars but _handle_mittari_start re-strips to 64 chars after the 'mittari_' prefix is removed. Slight inconsistency — collapse both to 64 to match the Pydantic max_length on _MittariSubscribePayload and the index expectation.

broadcast_mittari_state_change sends serially in an async for loop with `await send_message`. For 1000+ subscribers this is ~1000*15s worst case if Telegram rate-limits. Acceptable for current scale but document an upgrade path to asyncio.gather batched by 30 sends/sec (Telegram limit).

/api/webhooks/telegram audit-log insert wraps in try/except but logs full update result including pending_id — fine for debugging now but consider TTL on telegram_webhook_log collection so it doesn't grow unbounded. No TTL index exists today.

telegram_bot._STATE_LABEL_FI maps KYLMA→TYYNI etc but dial_engine's broadcast call passes the lowercased/raw state.key which is then upper()'d inside the function — works, but the contract should be explicit (either both sides UPPER or both sides handle case). Trivial.

VoitaRaffle endpoints rejected ALL 5 currently-seeded raffles as status='paid' — test_voita_start_binds_entry could not run. Consider seeding at least one 'active' raffle as part of CI bootstrap so the raffle binding path stays regression-tested.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter36_telegram_mittari.py (NEW — 21 tests covering full webhook dispatcher + mittari subscribe/binding/status + admin gating + module export sanity)
seed_data_creation: TEST_iter36_* prefixed pending_ids inserted into mittari_subscribers (6 rows: subscribe-only, bound, bound-without-subscribe, stop, unsubscribe, unknown lookup). Telegram_webhook_log entries auto-created (one per webhook hit, ~15 rows). All filterable by 'TEST_iter36_' prefix. No voita_entries created (all raffles status=paid).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint B Slices 3+4 complete and validated. Telegram bot module at /app/backend/telegram_bot.py is self-contained, no external deps beyond httpx. Webhook URL must be registered via POST /api/admin/telegram/set-webhook before live updates flow (currently NOT registered to this preview host — set_webhook would need a public HTTPS callback). To exercise the 'with picks' MittariSignals branch live, seed /api/odds/featured by ensuring odds_engine has picks with sharpness>=40 (currently empty). Telegram sendMessage failures with chat_not_found are EXPECTED and OK — the binding pipeline never raises. Reusable test: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter36_telegram_mittari.py -v. Iter35 fixture-leak failures in test_sprint_voita_hero.py remain pre-existing (not re-run).
rca of the issue: No defects found. All Slice 3 + Slice 4 acceptance criteria met. DialCockpit hero is byte-for-byte the same component (Mittari.jsx imports MittariSignals BETWEEN drivers section and method section — no edits to DialCockpit children).
